name: Connecticut Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Connecticut - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Department of Public Health — Speech-Language Pathology

address.description: Department of Public Health, Practitioner Licensing & Investigations Section, 410 Capitol Avenue, MS #12HLS, P.O. Box 340308, Hartford, CT 06134-0308

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Connecticut SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial fee is $200. Annual renewal is $150. Connecticut requires annual renewal.

mainEntity.name: Is Connecticut part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Connecticut has not enacted ASLP-IC legislation as of 2026.

mainEntity.name: Does Connecticut allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Connecticut permits telepractice but requires a valid Connecticut SLP license.

mainEntity.name: Does Connecticut have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Connecticut. To work in Connecticut, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Connecticut?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Connecticut while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
